summary: Funnel reveals a severe intake-to-purchase collapse (75 checkout views -> 27 paid in 30d) concentrated in medical-certificate, plus serious measurement integrity problems: Google Ads server conversions (100) and ads-reported conversions (22) wildly diverge from actual paid orders (27 total, 18 ads-attributed local), inflating CAC truth. Recovery email program is effectively dead (0 conversions from 119 partials). Request-to-checkout captures failed across all services with console errors, indicating a likely intake progression defect that warrants manual verification before scaling spend.

findings.blocker: Google Ads conversion signal is decoupled from real revenue: google_ads_server_conversion=100 and ads_conversions=22 vs only 27 total paid orders (18 ads-attributed) in 30d. purchase_completed (client)=6 vs purchase_completed_server=24 also disagree.
findings.evidence: PostHog 30d: google_ads_server_conversion=100, purchase_completed=6, purchase_completed_server=24; Ads 30d ads_conversions=22 local_orders=18; Supabase 30d paid=27 adAttributed=26
findings.cac_impact: Smart Bidding optimizes on inflated 100 conversions vs ~18-27 real; true ads_cac is $92.71 local but reported ads_cpa $75.85 understates by ~22%. Misfeeding signal sustains overspend on low-value clicks.
findings.recommended_fix: Audit the server conversion upload pipeline; reconcile google_ads_server_conversion to deduped purchase_completed_server. Stop counting non-purchase events as conversions. Align Ads conversion action to one verified server purchase event before any budget increase.

findings.blocker: All request-to-checkout mobile captures failed with consoleErrors=1, halting on /request URLs across med-cert, repeat-script, ED, and hair-loss.
findings.evidence: Browser Captures: 4x 'request-to-checkout mobile: failed ... consoleErrors=1' for medical-certificate, repeat-prescriptions, erectile-dysfunction, hair-loss
findings.cac_impact: If reproducible on production, this caps paid conversion regardless of ad spend; mobile is the dominant device (device=m in ad URLs). Could explain checkout_viewed=75 -> paid=27 drop.
findings.compliance_risk: medium - broken intake may present incomplete medical screening flow
findings.recommended_fix: Note: captures ran against localhost:3060, not production; manually reproduce on instantmed.com.au mobile before concluding outage. Capture the console error stack and the failing step. Block spend scaling until verified.

findings.blocker: Recovery/abandonment program produces zero conversions despite 119 partials and 6 recovery emails sent; 21 partials have email captured but no recovered purchase.
findings.evidence: Supabase 30d partial totals: count=119, converted=0, recoverySent=6, withEmail=21; abandonedEmailSent=8 at totals level
findings.affected_service: medical-certificate (75 of 119 partials at certificate step)
findings.cac_impact: 119 partials vs 27 paid = large recoverable pool. Even 10% recovery (~12 orders) at AOV ~$39.93 adds ~$479 net at near-zero CAC, materially lowering blended CAC.
findings.recommended_fix: Fix recovery email targeting/coverage: only 6 of 119 partials and 21 with email were contacted. Expand triggers to all email-captured partials, verify deliverability, add med-cert-specific recovery copy.

findings.blocker: Massive intake-to-checkout funnel drop: intake_started=384, step_completed=1308, checkout_viewed=75, paid=27 (30d). ~80% of those who view checkout do not pay.
findings.evidence: PostHog 30d: intake_started=384, checkout_viewed=75; Supabase paid=27, paidRate=0.7297; failedPayment=2, pendingPayment=1
findings.cac_impact: Only ~20% (75/384) reach checkout. Recovering intake->checkout to 30% could roughly double orders without added spend, halving effective CAC.
findings.recommended_fix: Instrument step-level drop-off between step_completed and checkout_viewed; identify which intake step (certificate step holds 75 partials) sheds users; reduce required fields / clarify pricing pre-checkout.

findings.blocker: Spend concentrated on medical-certificate keywords while higher-AOV services (ED $49.95, general $49.95) get near-zero ad attribution (adAttributed=0).
findings.evidence: Supabase 30d: med-cert adAttributed=25 AOV=$38.02; erectile-dysfunction adAttributed=0 AOV=$49.95; general adAttributed=0 AOV=$49.95
findings.cac_impact: Blended AOV is dragged to $39.93 by low-value med-cert. Shifting budget toward higher-AOV intents could improve revenue per conversion and lower CAC-to-LTV ratio.
findings.recommended_fix: Review campaign allocation; test budget toward ED/higher-AOV landing pages that currently get organic-only conversions. Validate landing pages convert before reallocating.

findings.blocker: Refunds concentrated and notable: general service refunded 1 of 2 orders (50%), 30d total refundAud=$79.90 on netAud=$998.25.
findings.evidence: Supabase 30d service general: paid=2 refunded=1 refundAud=49.95; totals refunded=2 refundAud=79.9
findings.cac_impact: High refund rate on general erodes net revenue and signals possible expectation mismatch; small volume limits dollar impact now but scales badly.
findings.compliance_risk: medium - refunds may indicate service/clinical expectation mismatch worth review
findings.recommended_fix: Review general-service refund reasons; clarify scope/eligibility on landing and pre-payment. Low volume so monitor rather than act on spend.

findings.blocker: Persistent requestFailures=1 on all landing page captures (desktop+mobile), excluding hair-loss mobile.
findings.evidence: Browser Captures: requestFailures=1 across med-cert/prescriptions/ED/hair-loss landings; attribution-continuity mobile requestFailures=3
findings.cac_impact: Likely a non-blocking asset/beacon failure given consoleErrors=0 and ok status, but a failed analytics/pixel request could undercount attribution.
findings.recommended_fix: Identify the failing request on landings; if it is a tracking pixel/beacon, fix to protect attribution accuracy. Cosmetic if it is a third-party asset.

ads_triage: Do not increase budgets until the conversion signal discrepancy (100 server conversions vs ~27 real paid) is reconciled - current Smart Bidding is likely optimizing on inflated data.
Recommend holding med-cert spend flat and adding negative review only after intake->checkout instrumentation identifies the drop step; no live mutation approved.
Consider capping spend on keywords feeding only med-cert until blended AOV/CAC improves; evaluate reallocation to ED/general after landing-page conversion is verified.
No live Ads mutations performed or approved in this audit.
measurement_notes: Conversion event chaos: purchase_completed (client)=6 vs purchase_completed_server=24 (30d) vs Supabase paid=27 - three different purchase counts; pick server-side as source of truth and dedupe.
google_ads_server_conversion=100 is identical in 30d and 90d windows, suggesting a stale/cached or misconfigured counter - verify the event is firing per-conversion and not a constant.
ads_conversions frozen at 22 across both 30d and 90d while spend differs ($1,668.80 vs $2,149.93) - conversion reporting may be stalled or capped; investigate Ads conversion import lag.
Browser captures for request-to-checkout ran against localhost:3060, not production instantmed.com.au - failures must be reproduced on production before treating as a live outage.
PostHog intake_funnel_payment_completed=83 (30d) far exceeds Supabase paid=27, indicating event-to-order mapping is unreliable; reconcile before trusting funnel ratios.
adAttributed=26 (30d) and =26 (90d) identical, mirroring the frozen ads metrics - attribution window or sync appears stuck.
